sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 52:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 52:1
items.html: Because it is opposite from the root. The form of the root is only to bestow, and that of phase four is only to receive. Two opposites cannot stem from one another by way of cause and consequence, but rather through a gradual, slow cascading of the degree, since the root is only the cause of phase one, the closest to it, phase one of phase two, and phase two of phase three. After phase three, it is possible for phase four to be revealed. (Inner Observation, Chapter One, Item 5)
